title: How to snap out of Evangelion
display_title: How to snap out of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: How to snap out of Evangelion. Post by NeverKnowsBest » Thu Aug 06, 2009 6:31 am Well when I went to Otakon this year I was fianlly about to watch a decent version of RoE: You Are (Not) Alone, and it completely struck a nerve. Before Otakon I was slowly drifting away from anime, and when I got back home I was into it more than anything else. As of right now I am obsessed with Evangelion. I have been buying every piece of Evangelion merchandise I can get my hands on (I bought the thin pack about the year ago, I finally got my copy of EoE like last week, and I purchased EVERY volume that is out in America as well....) At least once a day I do something Eva related. Like when I get home I'm probably going to continue making an online signature using a gif I made using ulead of the emotional "elevator scene" from EoE. Now here is the point of the topic. What can I do to snap out of this Evangelion craze? I was thinking about trying to find a new anime to draw my attention or something else. Please someone suggust something productive for me to do. Please don't give joke answers or flame. I think the reason why Evangelion is on my mind is because I keep seeing sometime Evangelion related at least once a week to hold my attention. At Otakon, I watch 1.0, the next week I started watching the series again, the week after I watched End of Evangelion again, this week I watch 2.0.

[]: Post by Fazmotron » Thu Aug 06, 2009 6:35 am YOU ARE ASKING THE WRONG PEOPLE! Seriously, this is the worst place to come if you want to get away from Eva.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Aug 06, 2009 6:55 am Fazmotron wrote: YOU ARE ASKING THE WRONG PEOPLE! Seriously, this is the worst place to come if you want to get away from Eva.

[]: Post by Agentomega » Thu Aug 06, 2009 7:09 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Fazmotron wrote: YOU ARE ASKING THE WRONG PEOPLE! Seriously, this is the worst place to come if you want to get away from Eva.

[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Aug 06, 2009 8:41 am Imagine Anno glaring unapprovingly at you and telling you to study harder every time you think about entertaining your nerdy little hobby. Should you fail to restrain yourself, a steady diet of shitty fanfics, fanwank threads, gross-out doujinshi (only those causing more emotional distress than sexual response are permitted), and Reichu's artwork should make you sick of the series in no time.

[]: Post by NeverKnowsBest » Thu Aug 06, 2009 8:52 am Reichu wrote: Imagine Anno glaring unapprovingly at you and telling you to study harder every time you think about entertaining your nerdy little hobby. Should you fail to restrain yourself, a steady diet of shitty fanfics, fanwank threads, gross-out doujinshi (only those causing more emotional distress than sexual response are permitted), and Reichu's artwork should make you sick of the series in no time.

[]: Post by Fazmotron » Thu Aug 06, 2009 8:55 am Quick, show him the naked Evas. It will either solidify his status as an EvaGeek, or send him screaming from the forums.

[]: Re: How to snap out of Evangelion. Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Aug 06, 2009 9:37 am NeverKnowsBest wrote: Now here is the point of the topic. What can I do to snap out of this Evangelion craze? I was thinking about trying to find a new anime to draw my attention or something else. Please someone suggust something productive for me to do.

[]: Post by schismatics » Thu Aug 06, 2009 9:52 am Just give it time. My Eva obsession comes in waves. I'm usually pretty regular to visiting this site, but I don't watch NGE all the time...just every few months or so.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Aug 06, 2009 9:57 am Who were you at Otakon, did we see you and if not then its a shame cause it would have been nice to meet you.

[]: Post by VoidEater » Thu Aug 06, 2009 10:29 am Ask yourself, Why am I obsessed? Are you natuarlly leaning towards obsessiveness in other parts of your life? If it's just NGE, is there something about it that you can see in something else (preferably Real Life People)? Can NGE be a part of your routine, with a fixed time range each day or week? Can you be disciplined about adhering to that schedule? Ask yoursefl, Why should I avoid this obsession? Is it interfering with other parts of my life, like working and paying rent and getting the dishes done? Is NGE filling a void in your life, or offering you something exciting? Is there a message you need to get? Once you "get it", can you hang up the phone? Can NGE have a normal spot in your life?

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Aug 06, 2009 10:33 am Fazmotron wrote: YOU ARE ASKING THE WRONG PEOPLE!

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Aug 06, 2009 10:36 am Hehe, your post should be a general NGE questionnaire, VoidEater! I know it wasn't directed at me, but I wanna play: Ask yourself, Why am I obsessed? -- Because NGE gave my entire life a kick in the ass when I saw it and I'm forever indebted to it for practically saving my life. Are you natuarlly leaning towards obsessiveness in other parts of your life? -- Oh yeah, I've always been the obsessive type. If it's just NGE, is there something about it that you can see in something else (preferably Real Life People)? -- I saw NGE as the perfect allegory for all the suffering in my life. Somehow it managed to encapsulate it all while, at the same time, offering me a means to find my way out of it. Can NGE be a part of your routine, with a fixed time range each day or week? Can you be disciplined about adhering to that schedule? -- Sure. I don't go out of my way to post about NGE. When I'm busy at work I might not get on here for a week or more. Ask yoursefl, Why should I avoid this obsession? Is it interfering with other parts of my life, like working and paying rent and getting the dishes done? -- Nah, I'm fine here. Is NGE filling a void in your life, or offering you something exciting? -- Well, it fills the same void all art does for me. Is there a message you need to get? Once you "get it", can you hang up the phone? -- I've gotten the message, but there's something about hearing it on repeat echoing on the voices of so many different individuals that keeps me coming back to hit "play". Can NGE have a normal spot in your life? - Yep. :)

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Aug 06, 2009 10:47 am Try getting into an activity or craft that reflects your obsession, but make it about something that isn't your obsession. That will open your interests up into other things. Draw up comics for the web or make videos. It will help you keep an open mind about other things since your not trying to do a comic about NGE. I've never really been all that obsessed over anime, but I really like the good animes out there, almost to the point of obsession at times. NGE is one of those animes. But I find that it keeps me from getting obsessed over one particular thing if I just use my obsession as a stepping stone into other things. Exmple: I was obsessed over Jurassic Park as a kid. So I was constantly thinking of making a dinosaur movie. I made videography my hobby in high school and turned it into a career choice in college. Now I'm actually working in the field. All of these movies or animes that I enjoy are now simply things that inspire me in my works. I see the various film and anime conventions as just networking opportunities for me to try and meet other people who work in the entertainment business. So just make a productive activity out of an obsession, and the rest will just write itself.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Aug 06, 2009 1:45 pm Fazmotron wrote: Quick, show him the naked Evas.

[]: Post by remix220 » Thu Aug 06, 2009 4:21 pm Fazmotron wrote: YOU ARE ASKING THE WRONG PEOPLE! Seriously, this is the worst place to come if you want to get away from Eva.

[]: Post by Fredrick2003 » Thu Aug 06, 2009 5:16 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Fazmotron wrote: Quick, show him the naked Evas.

[]: Post by i_Love_Asuka » Thu Aug 06, 2009 5:36 pm step 1: you are on a forum called "Evageeks" step 2: you already are an EVA-fan, step 3: ? step 4: profit! in one line: there is no hope for you anymore, you are an EVA-otaku now and you will remain an EVA-otaku, 'nuff said... or like Sammaeloo would say: "you're one of ussssss"

[]: Post by ath » Thu Aug 06, 2009 6:27 pm Misato, in episode #23, wrote: The tragedy of the people possessed by the Evas...

[]: Post by scarmullet » Thu Aug 06, 2009 6:28 pm Here is a suggestion, watch another Anime. Try RahXephon. Its just like Eva.
